Test Title/Summary of test cases: Confirm initializing the create job popup
Pre-conditions: Admin has successfully authenticated to the RADAR MD site.
Test Steps:
1. Access the RADAR site
2. Click on the Job Lists menu
3. Click on the [Create Crawl Job] button
Expected Result:
3. Open the Create a Craw Job popup:
- "Create a Crawl Job" label
- "Platform" dropdown: required, show "Select platform" placeholder
- "Job Type" dropdown: required, show "Select job type" placeholder
- [Create Crawl Job] button: enable
- [Cancel] button: enable
- [X] icon button: enable

Test Title/Summary of test cases: Confirm initializing the create job popup in case select "Get Products" job type
Pre-conditions:
Admin has successfully authenticated to the RADAR MD site.
The system has at least 1 created job.
Test Steps:
1. Access the RADAR site
2. Click on the Job Lists menu
3. Click on the [Create Crawl Job] button
4. Select "Get Products" for the Job Type field
Expected Result:
3. Open the Create a Craw Job popup.
4. The "Categories" dropdown is shown with the "Please select categories that you want to crawl products" placeholder.

Test Title/Summary of test cases: Verify that the admin can not create a crawl job without selecting platform
Pre-conditions: Admin has successfully authenticated to the RADAR MD site.
Test Steps:
1. Access the RADAR site
2. Click on the Job Lists menu
3. Click on the [Create Crawl Job] button
4. Select a value of the "Job type" field
5. Select a value of the "Categories" field if the selected value at step 4 is "Get products"
6. Click on [Create Crawl Job] button
Expected Result: 6. Show an inline message stating below the "Platform" field: "This field is required."

Test Title/Summary of test cases: Ensure that the admin can create a crawl job for OY platform to get categories
Pre-conditions:
Admin has successfully authenticated to the RADAR MD site.
The system has no running jobs.
Test Steps:
1. Access the RADAR site
2. Click on the Job Lists menu
3. Click on the [Create Crawl Job] button
4. Select "OliveYoung" value of the "Platform" field
5. Select "Get categories" value of the "Job type" field
6. Click on [Create a Crawl Job] button
Expected Result:
6. The Create a Crawl Job popup is closed, back to the Job Lists screen. The system show the success toast message "Job created successfully".
A new crawl job is created and show in the first of the job list table, with below information:
- Job ID: unique id is generated for the new job
- Platform: show "OliveYoung"
- Job Type: show "Get catigories"
- Status: "Running" if the system is available to run the job immediately, "Pending" if the system is running other jobs
- Start Date: created date time of the new crawl job
- End data: show "-"

Test Title/Summary of test cases: Verify that a job status changes from Pending to Running when execution starts.
Pre-conditions: A job is created and is in Pending status.
Test Steps:
1. The system execution job when available 
2. Check job status.
Expected Result: The job status updates to Running when execution starts.

Test Title/Summary of test cases: Ensure that the system updates the job status to Done when it completes successfully.
Pre-conditions: A job is in Running status.
Test Steps:
1. Let the job execute fully. 
2. Verify the final job status.
Expected Result: The job status updates to Done upon successful completion.

Test Title/Summary of test cases: Ensure that the system updates the job status to Failed if an error occurs.
Pre-conditions: A job is in Running status.
Test Steps:
1. Simulate a failure during job execution. 
2. Verify the final job status.
Expected Result: The job status updates to Failed if an error occurs.

Test Title/Summary of test cases: Verify that jobs can be created for both Get Categories and Get Products job types.
Pre-conditions: User is on the job creation form.
Test Steps:
1. Create a job with Get Categories type. 
2. Create a job with Get Products type. 
3. Check both jobs in the job list.
Expected Result: Jobs are successfully created with the correct job type.

Test Title/Summary of test cases: Ensure that the system does not allow creating duplicate jobs with the same parameters.
Pre-conditions: A job with the same type and platform already exists in Pending status.
Test Steps:
1. Try to create a job with the same type and platform. 
2. Check if the system allows it.
Expected Result: The system prevents duplicate job creation and displays an error message.

Test Title/Summary of test cases: Ensure that the newly created job appears in the job list.
Test Steps:
1. Create a new job. 
2. Navigate to the job list.
Expected Result: The new job is visible in the job list with the correct details.

Test Title/Summary of test cases: Ensure that a failed job can be retried.
Pre-conditions: A job is in Failed status.
Test Steps:
1. Locate the failed job. 
2. Click "Retry". 
3. Verify job status updates to Pending and re-executes.
Expected Result: The job status changes to Pending and runs again.

Test Title/Summary of test cases: Ensure that the admin can create crawl job to get products from many categories
Pre-conditions:
Admin has successfully authenticated to the RADAR MD site.
The system has no running jobs.
Test Steps:
1. Access the RADAR site
2. Click on the Job Lists menu
3. Click on the [Create Crawl Job] button
4. Select "OliveYoung" value of the "Platform" field
5. Select "Get products" value of the "Job type" field
6. Select all categories
7. Click on [Create Crawl Job]
Expected Result: The new job is visible in the job list with the correct details.
